strengths: 14+ years of full-stack production experience with TypeScript and Node.js spanning Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and current re:cinq role
Explicit AI agent development at re:cinq: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks directly match the listed AI agent requirement
Proven cloud and containerization depth: AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes for GISCollective, Docker across multiple roles
concerns: NestJS — the primary backend framework — is entirely absent from the resume despite extensive Node.js history
Kotlin is not mentioned anywhere, leaving the secondary backend stack requirement fully unaddressed
No explicit reference to Jest, Vitest, or Playwright; testing competency is inferred from TDD practice at RIDE Capital and ownership of a D-lang assertion library, not from the required frameworks
response.justification: Szabo's current re:cinq role explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, directly satisfying a differentiating requirement that many candidates will lack; his TypeScript and Node.js experience is deep and continuous across five roles over a decade. Cloud and containerization competency is concretely evidenced by AWS CDK at Optilyz and Kubernetes deployment for GISCollective. The most material gap is NestJS, which does not appear in the resume at all despite being the listed primary backend framework — his Node.js depth makes this learnable but adds ramp-up cost. Kotlin (secondary stack) and the specific JS testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright) are also absent, though TDD discipline at RIDE Capital and a published test-assertion library demonstrate testing culture. The AI agent and TypeScript breadth outweigh the framework gaps at this seniority level.
